nodes.name: ARCHITECTURE.md
nodes.summary: Describes the four runtime layers of the solution (host, WASM client, shared contracts, infrastructure) and the Azure reporting flow from AzureReportService through AzureReportStore and diagnostics endpoints to the hidden /azure dashboard.

nodes.name: CONVENTIONS.md
nodes.summary: Coding conventions for the project covering feature/infrastructure layout, immutable shared models, async and cancellation patterns, input validation, opt-in destructive Azure operations, and UI styling tokens.

nodes.name: PoPunkouterSoftware.sln
nodes.summary: Visual Studio solution file aggregating the host, client, shared, infrastructure, and three test projects (unit, integration, E2E) with their build configurations.
nodes.languageNotes: MSBuild solution file enumerating projects and per-configuration build mappings.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Top-level project overview documenting the solution layout, local development prerequisites and commands, runtime notes (health/diagnostics endpoints, Azure behavior), testing strategy, and developer guidance.

nodes.name: STACK.md
nodes.summary: Enumerates the technology stack: .NET 10 / C# 14, ASP.NET Core with Blazor WebAssembly, Radzen.Blazor, Azure SDKs, Serilog/OpenTelemetry, and the xUnit/Playwright test tooling.

nodes.name: TESTING.md
nodes.summary: Describes the testing strategy across unit, integration (WebApplicationFactory in the Testing environment), and Playwright E2E tests, plus required build verification and runtime checks for browser-facing changes.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose definition running an Azurite container that emulates Azure Blob, Queue, and Table Storage on ports 10000-10002 for local development and integration tests, backed by a named volume.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-service compose stack exposing Azurite storage emulator endpoints with a persistent named volume.

nodes.name: global.json
nodes.summary: Pins the .NET SDK to version 10.0.300 with latestMinor roll-forward, ensuring consistent build tooling across environments.
